expect: Composite mission score computable from existing discrete scores — weighted geometric mean of 8 sub-scores correlates >0.7 with human-judged mission health. Discrete-continuous gap <0.10 at S471.
actual: Composite IS computable (already exists in eval_sufficiency.py). Gap NOT <0.10: measured 0.196 at S471 — 5.8x previous record. Root cause: 3/4 goals simultaneously at discrete boundary (continuous 2.70-2.87, all rounding to discrete=2). 67% of gap from hardcoded external_grounding=False ceilings (L-455). Reconciliation mechanism (`>1` threshold) structurally blind to exactly-1-step divergences. Historical gap range: -0.031 to +0.196 (n=7 snapshots S418-S471).
diff: Expected gap <0.10: got 0.196 (FALSIFIED — gap 2x prediction). Expected geometric mean as improvement: arithmetic mean already exists, geometric mean would not fix the discrete ceiling problem. The real finding is architectural: dual-representation systems accumulate divergence at boundaries, and the boundary-detection mechanism has a blind spot for exactly-1-step gaps. L-1173 filed. Prescription: use continuous_composite for overall verdict in mature phase (all continuous > 2.0).
self_apply: eval architecture improvements feed back into measuring swarm own evaluation quality — L-1173 prescribes using higher-fidelity signal for self-assessment, which is itself a higher-level evaluation decision
